meta.title: What's happening
meta.note: Curated cross-domain event log: the developments moving Mexico, each rewritten in plain English, dated, and linked to the original publisher or record source. Auto-generated from the news wire on every refresh. Raw or non-English items are quarantined and never published.

events.title: Moody's warns on falling federal transfers to states as welfare spending rises
events.why: Federal revenue shared with states grew just 0.9% annually between January and May, even as scholarship and pension programs squeeze the finance ministry's budget.

coverage.title: Becas y pensiones presionan a Hacienda; Moody’s alerta por caída en transferencias a estados
coverage.summary: Entre enero y mayo, la Recaudación Federal Participable tuvo un crecimiento de apenas 0.9% anual. La publicación Becas y pensiones presionan a Hacienda; Moody’s alerta por caída en transferencias a estados apareció primero en EL CEO .

events.background: The Recaudación Federal Participable (RFP) is the pool of federal tax revenue Mexico shares with states and municipalities, and welfare programs like scholarships and pensions draw on the same federal budget.
events.drivers: Moody's Local points to slowing national growth and the resulting slowdown in RFP collection, while spending on social transfers keeps rising and squeezing Hacienda's operating budget.
events.implications: Moody's says the mismatch between slowing revenue and rising operating spending could shrink operating margins for states and municipalities by the end of 2026.

events.title: FEMSA pushes Spin to become a digital Oxxo to rival Nu and Revolut
events.why: FEMSA's leadership wants its Spin fintech unit to replicate the scale of its Oxxo convenience-store network in digital finance, positioning it against fast-growing digital banks operating in Mexico.

coverage.title: ‘Oxxo Bank’: FEMSA quiere hacer de Spin un Oxxo digital para competir con Nu y Revolut
coverage.summary: La instrucción que Rodrigo García Jacques recibió de la dirección de FEMSA cuando tomó las riendas de Spin cabe en una sola frase: “hagamos otro Oxxo en digital”. Está consciente del peso de esa encomienda, porque –asegura– la cadena de tiendas de conveniencia redefinió el retail en México. Cumplir el mandato exigió pr

events.drivers: FEMSA leadership told Spin's head to 'do another Oxxo digitally,' pushing the firm to merge Spin, Oxxo and credit into one financial system able to match Nu, Revolut and Mercado Pago.
events.implications: The push targets credit, a segment García Jacques says neither neobanks nor traditional banks have solved.
events.background: FEMSA operates Oxxo, Mexico's largest convenience-store chain with more than 24,000 stores, alongside Spin, a fintech launched in 2021 that grew separately until early 2026.

events.title: Mexico's economy seen growing 1.7% in June, led by services
events.why: Services drove the gain while investment remained the main drag, according to early estimates ahead of INEGI's formal second-quarter data.

coverage.title: Perfilan crecimiento económico en México de 1.7 por ciento en junio y mejoría en el segundo trimestre
coverage.summary: Servicios, el principal motor del crecimiento, y la inversión, la mayor limitante.

events.background: INEGI's IOAE is an early, unofficial estimate of economic activity published ahead of the formal quarterly GDP report, used to gauge momentum before official data lands.
events.drivers: May's estimated growth of 1.6% and April's 2.2% feed into a projected 1.8% for the second quarter, well above the first quarter's 0.3%.
events.implications: The acceleration would mark the strongest quarterly pace in about two years and lift first-half growth to 1.1% from 0.4% a year earlier.

events.title: USTR recommends tighter USMCA auto rules of origin
events.why: The trade office cites a shrinking US content share in vehicles assembled in Mexico and Canada and regional reliance on chips and circuit boards sourced outside North America.

coverage.title: USTR Presses for Tighter Auto Rules of Origin in USMCA
coverage.summary: USTR has recommended tightening USMCA automotive rules of origin, citing a shrinking US content share in vehicles assembled in Mexico and Canada and regional dependence on chips, circuit boards and display panels from non-market economies. The push arrives as the treaty enters annual reviews through 2036 and Mexico fac

events.background: USMCA rules of origin set the regional content vehicles need to qualify for tariff-free treatment, and the pact now undergoes annual reviews after Washington declined a 16-year extension.
events.drivers: USTR points to a shrinking US content share in vehicles built in Mexico and Canada and to regional reliance on chips, circuit boards and display panels from non-market economies.
events.implications: Mexico faces 25% duties on vehicles and parts that fail to meet the qualifying content threshold.

events.title: Forced-labor complaint over Chinese camera imports tests USMCA mechanism
events.why: Civil groups file what they call the first citizen-driven case under the trade pact, accusing Mexico of allowing imports linked to forced labor, a claim that could trigger formal review procedures with the US.

coverage.title: El caso de las videocámaras chinas y trabajo forzoso que pone a México bajo escrutinio del T-MEC
coverage.summary: Organizaciones civiles señalan que el caso es el primero surgido desde la ciudadanía en México para intentar activar mecanismos previstos en el tratado comercial.

events.background: Article 23.6 of USMCA lets citizens file complaints over forced-labor imports, and the US has proposed a 10% minimum tariff for most partners on goods linked to forced labor.
events.drivers: Civil groups ProDESC and Empower revived a complaint over Dahua and Hikvision surveillance cameras tied to Xinjiang supply chains, filed with the labor ministry in 2023 and dismissed that same year.
events.implications: The case adds to US scrutiny of Mexico during the T-MEC review at the same time Mexico is trying to avoid the proposed 10% forced-labor tariff.

events.title: Chedraui profit falls 11.5% on US immigration crackdown
events.why: The retailer says fewer Hispanic shoppers visited its US supermarkets as immigration enforcement intensified, compounded by exchange-rate pressure on its earnings.

coverage.title: La política migratoria en EU golpea a Chedraui y sus ganancias caen 11.5% en el segundo trimestre
coverage.summary: La minorista resintió una menor afluencia de clientes en sus supermercados para consumidores hispanos en Estados Unidos, donde la presión migratoria y el tipo de cambio debilitaron sus ingresos.

events.background: Chedraui operates supermarkets in both Mexico and the United States, with its US stores serving largely Hispanic communities.

events.title: Walmart to cut Mexico workweek to 40 hours starting 2027
events.why: The move follows a legal requirement for Mexican companies to reduce standard hours from 48 to 40, with a phase-in period beginning this year.

coverage.title: Walmart reducirá jornada laboral en México a partir de 2027
coverage.summary: Las empresas mexicanas tendrán que reducir las horas de trabajo de las 48 a 40 horas a partir del próximo año, con un proceso de adaptación durante este año. La publicación Walmart reducirá jornada laboral en México a partir de 2027 apareció primero en EL CEO .

events.background: Mexican labor law currently sets a standard 48-hour workweek, and a reform pushed by the Sheinbaum government requires companies to cut it to 40 hours in stages between 2027 and 2030.
events.drivers: The labor ministry set 2026 as the year for companies to adjust their operations, and Walmart says it tested different operating models and productivity initiatives before committing to the January 2027 start.
events.implications: An EY report found 72% of companies in the Mexican market have not yet taken specific steps to adapt to the shorter workweek.
events.next: The reduction will phase in gradually by two hours a year until reaching 40 hours in 2030.

events.title: AT&T Mexico loses 668,000 prepaid users under phone registry rule
events.why: A new requirement linking mobile lines to user identity cut into AT&T's prepaid base, though postpaid growth kept revenue and cash flow rising.

coverage.title: AT&T pierde 668,000 usuarios de prepago por la política del registro telefónico
coverage.summary: La nueva política de vinculación de líneas afectó la base de clientes de prepago de AT&T México, pero el crecimiento del pospago permitió a la empresa mantener el avance de sus ingresos y del flujo operativo.

events.background: AT&T Mexico is one of the country's major mobile carriers, competing under a national registry rule that requires phone lines to be linked to verified user identity.

events.title: Mexico files 20 legal complaints over deaths of nationals in ICE custody
events.why: The foreign ministry expands a legal offensive announced two weeks ago after 17 Mexicans died in US custody, with eight complaints directed at state prosecutors.

coverage.title: El Gobierno detalla que ha presentado 20 denuncias por la muerte de migrantes a manos del ICE
coverage.summary: La Cancillería ha aportado datos adicionales sobre la ofensiva contra Washington anunciada hace dos semanas en respuesta a las muertes de 17 mexicanos. Ocho denuncias se han dirigido a fiscalías estatales y otras 12 en condados

events.title: Security chief says Mexico has not located El Mayo's properties
events.why: García Harfuch says it is up to the attorney general's office to decide on an extradition request for the jailed Sinaloa Cartel leader, who wants to serve part of his sentence in Mexico.

coverage.title: García Harfuch admite que no han encontrado propiedades del Mayo en México
coverage.summary: El capo pide cumplir parte de la cadena perpetua en su país. El secretario de Seguridad asegura que es la Fiscalía quien tendría que determinar si solicita su orden de extradición

events.drivers: Zambada's lawyer says the 76-year-old wants to serve part of his sentence in Mexico and will not comply with the asset forfeiture order.
events.implications: García Harfuch says Mexican authorities have seized accounts and properties linked to the Sinaloa Cartel, but none registered under Zambada's name.

events.title: Sheinbaum takes cautious stance on possible new US tariffs against 60 countries
events.why: The economy ministry says it presented arguments to US officials for why Mexico should be excluded from any new round of tariffs.

coverage.title: Claudia Sheinbaum toma con cautela posibles nuevos aranceles de EU contra 60 países
coverage.summary: Claudia Sheinbaum afirmó que la SE de economía sostuvo una audiencia con EU donde expusó los argumentos para que México no reciba más aranceles La publicación Claudia Sheinbaum toma con cautela posibles nuevos aranceles de EU contra 60 países apareció primero en EL CEO .

events.background: USTR Jamieson Greer has authority under Section 301 of the US Trade Act to impose tariffs on countries linked to forced-labor production or unfair trade practices.
events.drivers: Greer told CNBC the new tariffs would target roughly 60 countries where goods are found to involve forced labor or unfair commercial practices.
events.next: Sheinbaum says her government will wait for an official announcement from Washington before knowing whether Mexico is among the countries targeted.

events.title: Citi survey sees peso weakening toward 17.92 per dollar by year-end
events.why: The forecast ties the currency's expected slide to uncertainty over the USMCA review and anticipated Banxico moves in the second half of the year.
events.background: Banxico targets 3% inflation and sets its policy rate partly to keep Mexican rates above US levels, a differential that supports the peso and that investors watch closely.
events.drivers: Citi's July survey points to depreciation as USMCA negotiations create uncertainty and analysts anticipate shifts in the Banxico-Fed rate gap.
events.implications: The consensus implies the peso would give back part of its 2.86% first-half gain, closing 2026 near 17.92 pesos per dollar versus 17.49 at midyear.

coverage.title: Citi survey sees peso weakening toward 17.92 per dollar by year-end
coverage.summary: The forecast ties the currency's expected slide to uncertainty over the USMCA review and anticipated Banxico moves in the second half of the year.

events.title: Sheinbaum presses USMCA talks with Trump and Carney at World Cup final
events.why: The brief in-person exchange coincided with the opening of a new round of trilateral trade talks in Mexico City on the treaty's review.
events.background: USMCA links Mexico, the US and Canada, and after the first joint review on 1 July 2026 the US declined a 16-year extension, so the pact now faces annual reviews under Article 34.7 through 2036.
events.drivers: Washington's new 50% tariff on Canadian goods, applied even to treaty-compliant products, is pushing Mexico to seek elimination of duties on its own rules-of-origin-compliant exports.
events.next: Sheinbaum was preparing to host USTR Jamieson Greer in Mexico City as the new round of talks opened.
events.implications: The Canadian tariff, applied despite compliance with the treaty, shows Washington willing to tax partner goods even under USMCA rules.

events.title: Officials report Asian imports fell 23.2% after Mexico’s tariffs
events.why: Officials say the drop in imports from Asia strengthens Mexico's position in the USMCA review by showing reduced reliance on non-regional suppliers.
events.background: In December 2025 Mexico imposed tariffs on imports from countries without a free trade agreement, aiming to curb the Chinese-origin components that flow through its factories despite USMCA rules of origin.
events.drivers: Between January and May, imports under those tariff lines from non-FTA countries fell 23.2% year-on-year, with China's drop at 28.4%, Taiwan's at 32.7% and South Korea's at 21.9%.
events.implications: Despite the broad decline, Chinese heavy trucks remain a separate obstacle to Mexico securing exemption from Trump's tariffs.

coverage.title: Officials report Asian imports fell 23.2% after Mexico’s tariffs
coverage.summary: Officials say the drop in imports from Asia strengthens Mexico's position in the USMCA review by showing reduced reliance on non-regional suppliers.

events.title: Mexico is not among the leading destinations for private AI investment
events.why: The US and Canada rank among the top global recipients of private AI investment while Mexico does not appear on the list, despite all three being partners in the same trade pact.
events.background: Mexico, the US and Canada are USMCA partners, and Stanford's AI Index Report 2026 tracks which countries attract private capital for AI development as computing infrastructure needs grow.
events.implications: The index reported 173 data centers in Mexico at the end of 2025, compared with 337 in Canada and 5,427 in the US.
events.next: Watch new data-center projects and Mexico's position in the next AI Index to see whether the gap narrows.
events.drivers: The US led global private AI investment with $285,880 million in 2025, and Canada also ranked among the top five, while Mexico shows only a rise in graduates trained for technology development rather than investment.

coverage.title: Mexico is not among the leading destinations for private AI investment
coverage.summary: The US and Canada rank among the top global recipients of private AI investment while Mexico does not appear on the list, despite all three being partners in the same trade pact.

events.title: Questions surround the death of Juan Jairo Coronilla, the Mexican who was fleeing ICE: ‘He came with his work visa’
events.why: The 28-year-old from Guanajuato worked as a furniture salesman and had a return ticket to Mexico for July 18.

coverage.title: Questions surround the death of Juan Jairo Coronilla, the Mexican who was fleeing ICE: ‘He came with his work visa’
coverage.summary: The 28-year-old from Guanajuato worked as a furniture salesman and had a return ticket to Mexico for July 18.

events.title: US imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports, straining North American trade pact
events.why: The tariff, applied under Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act, hits Canadian goods even when they meet USMCA content rules, adding pressure to the trilateral review Mexico is also negotiating.

coverage.title: US imposes 50% tariff on Canadian imports, straining North American trade pact
coverage.summary: The tariff, applied under Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act, hits Canadian goods even when they meet USMCA content rules, adding pressure to the trilateral review Mexico is also negotiating.

events.background: Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act lets Washington impose duties even on goods that meet a trade agreement's content rules, and about 85% of Mexico's exports currently enter the US tariff-free under USMCA.
events.drivers: Washington has escalated tariff actions against Canada even where USMCA content rules are met, adding to existing Section 232 duties on steel, aluminum and automotive goods.
events.implications: The move sets a precedent that could be applied to Mexico's own tariff-free access under the same agreement.

events.title: Banxico and CNBV work to manage risks as fintechs convert into banks
events.why: Regulators are responding as fintech firms obtain banking licenses in Mexico, intensifying competition with incumbent lenders.

coverage.title: Banxico and CNBV work to manage risks as fintechs convert into banks
coverage.summary: Regulators are responding as fintech firms obtain banking licenses in Mexico, intensifying competition with incumbent lenders.

events.background: Banxico, the CNBV and the finance ministry jointly authorize fintechs seeking to convert into full banks, a process now underway for several firms.
events.drivers: FinTech México's Felipe Vallejo says a wave of fintechs converting into banks is pushing incumbent banks to move faster and regulators to weigh the risks of more remote, tech-driven competition.
events.implications: Vallejo frames the shift as producing "only good things" through a competitive cycle among banks and authorities.

events.title: Nu begins operating as a bank in Mexico as Banorte welcomes competition
events.why: The Brazilian fintech's move into full banking status intensifies competition in Mexico's banking sector, according to Banorte executives.

coverage.title: Nu begins operating as a bank in Mexico as Banorte welcomes competition
coverage.summary: The Brazilian fintech's move into full banking status intensifies competition in Mexico's banking sector, according to Banorte executives.

events.background: Mexico's banking sector has a bank credit-to-GDP ratio of about 35.5%, low for a large economy, leaving room for fintechs to convert into full banks under multiple-banking licenses.
events.drivers: Nu México just received regulatory approval for a multiple-banking license, joining Plata and Revolut, which already operate as banks, as fintechs push to compete on equal regulatory footing.
events.next: Mercado Pago, Konfío and Bunq are still awaiting approval to convert to full banking licenses.
events.implications: Banorte's director said the entry of newly licensed fintechs increases competition under the same rules as established banks.

events.title: Mexico rules out domestic cyclospora outbreak, coordinates with US on source
events.why: Mexico's health secretary said the country is not experiencing an outbreak but is working with US authorities to trace the source of an infection reaching record levels in the United States.

coverage.title: Cyclospora y diarrea explosiva: lo que hay que saber sobre los tres casos detectados en México y el brote en Estados Unidos
coverage.summary: El secretario de Salud descarta un brote en territorio nacional y confirma que México colabora con EE UU para determinar el origen de la infección, que alcanza niveles récord en ese país

events.background: Cyclospora is a microscopic parasite causing cyclosporiasis, an intestinal illness usually linked to contaminated fresh fruit and vegetables.
events.drivers: A record surge of cases in the United States prompted Mexican and US authorities to investigate whether the outbreak's source lies in Mexico, after Mexico confirmed three cases of its own.
events.implications: Health secretary David Kershenobich says the three Mexican cases fall within normal epidemiology and show no evidence tying Mexico to the US outbreak.
events.next: Mexico says it will continue working with US authorities to determine the outbreak's origin.

events.title: Mexico raises 13 concerns in third round of USMCA review talks
events.why: Mexico's economy ministry pressed its list of trade concerns with US Trade Representative officials as the two countries work to preserve tariff advantages ahead of the pact's renewal decision.

coverage.title: Mexico raises 13 concerns in third round of USMCA review talks
coverage.summary: Mexico's economy ministry pressed its list of trade concerns with US Trade Representative officials as the two countries work to preserve tariff advantages ahead of the pact's renewal decision.

coverage.title: Mexico raises 13 concerns as USMCA review talks open in Mexico City
coverage.summary: The Economy Ministry presented 13 issues to the US Trade Representative's office as the first annual USMCA review round began, a formal step that will shape tariffs, auto rules and market access for years.

events.implications: Mexico's economy ministry says it will focus on getting the Trump administration to eliminate tariffs among the three member countries.
events.background: The first annual USMCA review requires the US, Mexico and Canada to renegotiate terms yearly rather than through the 16-year renewal Washington declined, with Mexico seeking to keep the tariff edge that made it the top exporter to the US.
events.next: Mexico said it will press the Trump administration to eliminate tariffs among the three member countries.

events.title: Mexico's economy grows 1.7% in June, boosted by World Cup spending, INEGI estimates
events.why: INEGI's timely indicator points to a monthly pickup tied largely to World Cup-related consumption, even as industrial output stays weak and investment lags.

coverage.title: Mundial impulsa a la economía mexicana: perfila avance de 1.7% en junio, según el Inegi
coverage.summary: El indicador oportuno de Inegi anticipa que la economía de México caiga 0.3% mensual en mayo, lastrada por una contracción industrial. La publicación Mundial impulsa a la economía mexicana: perfila avance de 1.7% en junio, según el Inegi apareció primero en EL CEO .

events.background: INEGI's IOAE indicator gives an early five-week estimate of monthly GDP activity ahead of the official IGAE data, after Mexico's economy contracted 0.6% quarterly in the first three months of 2026.
events.drivers: World Cup-related consumption lifted the June estimate to 1.7% annual growth from 1.6% in May, while industrial activity contracted 0.8% in the secondary sector in May.
events.implications: The pickup signals a partial recovery in activity heading into the second half of the year, but industrial weakness persists even as services strengthen.

events.title: Fuel theft network tied to Ruffo moved 800 million liters of gasoline in 15 months
events.why: Prosecutors say the scheme linked to former Baja California governor Ernesto Ruffo cost the treasury billions of pesos, one of the largest fuel-tax fraud cases to reach the federal attorney general's office.

coverage.title: Fuel theft network tied to Ruffo moved 800 million liters of gasoline in 15 months
coverage.summary: Prosecutors say the scheme linked to former Baja California governor Ernesto Ruffo cost the treasury billions of pesos, one of the largest fuel-tax fraud cases to reach the federal attorney general's office.

events.background: Huachicol fiscal refers to schemes that evade fuel taxes by falsifying import declarations, a long-running drain on Mexico's treasury.

events.title: Grupo México profit jumps 90.9% in second quarter on copper prices
events.why: The mining group controlled by Germán Larrea posted net profit of over 2 billion dollars for the quarter, driven by higher copper prices and adding to its interest in rail infrastructure abroad.

coverage.title: Grupo México ‘hace su agosto’ con el cobre: Ganancias se disparan 90.9% en el segundo trimestre de 2026
coverage.summary: Durante el segundo trimestre de 2026, Grupo México, el gigante minero propiedad de Germán Larrea, reportó una utilidad neta de 2 mil 93.14 millones de dólares.

events.background: Grupo México, controlled by Germán Larrea, is a mining conglomerate that also holds rail and transport businesses, making it one of the country's largest industrial groups.
events.drivers: Global copper demand rose and the company's cash cost per pound fell 56.7% in the second quarter compared with a year earlier.

events.title: América Móvil buys Peru's WOW to expand in Latin America
events.why: The Carlos Slim-controlled company agreed to acquire all shares of WOW from Liberty Latin America and Narvik Capital Partners, extending its fiber-optic network into Peru.

coverage.title: América Móvil, de Carlos Slim, adquiere WOW en Perú y acelera su expansión en América Latina
coverage.summary: América Móvil anunció que llegó a un acuerdo con Liberty Latin America y Narvik Capital Partners para adquirir el 100% de las acciones de WOW. La publicación América Móvil, de Carlos Slim, adquiere WOW en Perú y acelera su expansión en América Latina apareció primero en EL CEO .

events.background: América Móvil, Carlos Slim's telecom group, already operates in Peru through a local subsidiary and is expanding fiber-optic infrastructure across Latin America.
events.drivers: The company reached a deal with Liberty Latin America and Narvik Capital Partners to buy all of WOW, a Peruvian fiber-optic operator, without disclosing the price.
events.next: The transaction's close depends on approval from Peru's competition authority, Indecopi.
events.implications: The deal came just ahead of América Móvil's second-quarter earnings report, with shares up 0.22% to 22.80 pesos after the announcement.

events.title: ATI Materials commits $80 million to new Chihuahua plant
events.why: The US materials producer will open a forged-products division in Chihuahua City, adding to a wave of manufacturing investment along the northern border.

coverage.title: ATI Materials commits $80 million to new Chihuahua plant
coverage.summary: The US materials producer will open a forged-products division in Chihuahua City, adding to a wave of manufacturing investment along the northern border.

events.drivers: ATI committed $80 million to open a forged-products division, ATI Forged Products, in Chihuahua City, announced jointly by Governor Maru Campos and CEO Kimberly Fields at the Farnborough International Airshow.
events.implications: The project adds advanced forging and manufacturing capacity to the northern border region's aerospace-linked industrial base.
events.background: Northern Mexico, including Chihuahua, is a hub for export manufacturing tied to US supply chains.

events.title: Ferrero to invest $86 million to expand Guanajuato plant
events.why: The investment will expand export capacity at a site that already ships about 40% of the company's regional production, as Ferrero aims to double its Mexico business within six years.

coverage.title: Ferrero to invest $86 million to expand Guanajuato plant
coverage.summary: The investment will expand export capacity at a site that already ships about 40% of the company's regional production, as Ferrero aims to double its Mexico business within six years.

events.background: Ferrero, the Italian confectioner behind Nutella and Kinder, runs a manufacturing plant in Guanajuato that already exports about 40% of its regional production.

events.title: América Móvil acquires Peruvian telecom WOW from Liberty Latin America
events.why: The deal gives Carlos Slim's company full ownership of a fiber-optic network spanning Peru's 24 departments, extending its Latin American footprint.

coverage.title: Carlos Slim extiende el ‘territorio Telcel’: Compra empresa de telecomunicaciones en Perú
coverage.summary: Wow Perú, la nueva compañía propiedad de Slim, tiene una red basada de fibra óptica que se extiende por los 24 departamentos del país.

events.background: Wow Perú built an all-fiber optic network across Peru's 24 departments over five years, mostly outside Lima, where about 48.7% of the country's fixed internet connections are concentrated.
events.drivers: América Móvil, through its Peru subsidiary, agreed to buy the network entirely from Liberty Latin America and Narvik Capital Partners to extend Carlos Slim's telecom holdings there.
events.implications: The purchase gives América Móvil a foothold in regions outside Lima, where fixed broadband penetration has lagged.
events.next: Closing is subject to approval from Peru's antitrust regulator, Indecopi.

events.title: Mexican FIBRAs surpass MX$1 trillion in assets at 15-year mark
events.why: The real estate investment trust sector now represents about 4% of national GDP, driven largely by industrial property demand tied to nearshoring.

coverage.title: Mexican FIBRAs surpass MX$1 trillion in assets at 15-year mark
coverage.summary: The real estate investment trust sector now represents about 4% of national GDP, driven largely by industrial property demand tied to nearshoring.

events.drivers: Growth is tied mainly to expanding industrial and logistics demand linked to North American nearshoring.
events.implications: The MX$1 trillion scale affects institutional investors, pension funds (AFOREs) and multinational tenants expanding manufacturing operations.
events.background: FIBRAs are Mexico's real estate investment trusts, a market segment distinct from the narrow 35-company IPC stock index.

events.title: US and Mexico open first annual USMCA review
events.why: The two governments meet after Washington declined to renew the trade pact for another 16-year term, with steel, autos and agriculture as the main issues on the table.

coverage.title: US and Mexico kick off USMCA’s first annual review
coverage.summary: In their first face‑to‑face after Washington’s refusal to renew the trade agreement for another 16 years, the countries will address key sectors such as steel, automobiles and agriculture

events.background: USMCA's first annual review follows Washington's decision not to renew the pact for another 16 years, putting the treaty on a yearly review track through 2036.
events.drivers: Technical teams from both countries began work sessions Tuesday, and Sheinbaum met briefly with Trump and Carney at the World Cup closing ceremony before the review began.
events.implications: US officials say the talks will also cover economic security, labor issues and electronic payment services alongside autos, steel, aluminum and agriculture.
events.next: Sheinbaum is scheduled to meet USTR Jamieson Greer at the National Palace on Wednesday.

events.title: PRI proposes reform to curb excessive bank credit card fees
events.why: The party will push changes to the Credit Institutions Law aimed at limiting commissions and interest charges banks apply to credit cards.
events.background: Mexico's commercial banks lend little to the private sector relative to GDP, and consumer credit cards carry among the highest rates in that market, leaving households exposed to steep charges.
events.drivers: PRI coordinator Rubén Moreira cited credit products charging annual interest above 100% and total annual costs (CAT) above 200%, plus fees for not using a card, paying in cash or minor late payments.
events.next: The initiative will be discussed in the next legislative session.
events.implications: The reform would restrict several fee practices banks currently use to generate revenue from cardholders.

events.title: Profile examines Isabel Miranda de Wallace’s search for her missing son
events.why: The Guardian traces how Miranda de Wallace responded after her son disappeared and pursued a public campaign for justice.

events.title: Mexico and the US launch the first annual USMCA review
events.why: The two countries hold their first face-to-face talks since Washington declined to renew the pact for another 16 years, covering rules of origin, economic security and sectors including steel, autos and agriculture.
events.background: USMCA's first joint review followed Washington's refusal to extend the pact 16 more years, triggering annual reviews that will run alongside existing trade under the treaty.
events.drivers: Washington wants to cover economic security, labor rules, electronic payment services and trade in autos, steel, aluminum and agriculture in the talks.
events.next: A meeting between Sheinbaum and USTR's Jamieson Greer is set for Thursday at the National Palace.

coverage.title: Mexico and the US launch the first annual USMCA review
coverage.summary: The two countries hold their first face-to-face talks since Washington declined to renew the pact for another 16 years, covering rules of origin, economic security and sectors including steel, autos and agriculture.

events.title: USTR says the US is preparing additional tariffs
events.why: Washington has already floated roughly 10% duties on more than 80 countries as a first step before additional tariffs, a signal for exporters watching the USMCA review.
events.background: USMCA's annual review comes as the U.S. Supreme Court has already struck down much of the broad tariff program from Trump's second term, prompting Washington to seek firmer legal grounds for new duties.
events.next: Washington is preparing a visit to Mexico tied to the review.
events.implications: A further round targeting more than 40 nations over subsidies and currency manipulation is also being prepared, a signal for exporters watching the USMCA review.
events.drivers: USTR's Jamieson Greer said the administration is rebuilding the invalidated tariffs on sturdier legal justification, starting with roughly 10% duties on more than 80 countries over forced-labor and coercive labor practices.

events.title: Mayo Zambada receives a life sentence
events.why: A US court sentenced the Sinaloa Cartel co-founder to life in prison. The cartel's successor factions remain active.
events.background: The Sinaloa Cartel, once led by Zambada and Joaquín 'El Chapo' Guzmán, has been one of Mexico's most powerful and violent criminal organizations for decades.
events.drivers: Zambada's life sentence caps a rise through Culiacán's criminal networks that began in the 1970s, when he grew up in El Alamo near the city.
events.implications: The article suggests nostalgia for a calmer old cartel era is misleading, since that period was itself violent and unstable.

events.title: US says its campaign against Mexican corruption does not end with Mayo's sentencing
events.why: Washington argues organized crime and government in Mexico remain intertwined, while Sheinbaum demands respect for sovereignty and coordination without submission.
events.drivers: DEA director Terrance Cole says the sentences mark a milestone, not the end, in Washington's pursuit of narco-political corruption ties, while Sheinbaum insists on respect for Mexican sovereignty.

coverage.title: US says its campaign against Mexican corruption does not end with Mayo's sentencing
coverage.summary: Washington argues organized crime and government in Mexico remain intertwined, while Sheinbaum demands respect for sovereignty and coordination without submission.

events.implications: Washington maintains that organized crime and government in Mexico remain inseparable, keeping Mexico under continued US pressure over corruption despite the sentencing.

events.title: Alsea's profit falls 52.1% on weak Mexican consumption
events.why: Currency effects and soft consumer spending in Mexico offset gains at Chili's and Vips from World Cup traffic, dragging down the restaurant operator's consolidated results.
events.background: Alsea operates restaurant brands including Chili's and Vips in Mexico, where consumer spending has been weak alongside GDP growth of only about 0.6% in 2025.

coverage.title: Alsea's profit falls 52.1% on weak Mexican consumption
coverage.summary: Currency effects and soft consumer spending in Mexico offset gains at Chili's and Vips from World Cup traffic, dragging down the restaurant operator's consolidated results.

events.title: Mexican migrant dies fleeing an ICE operation in the US
events.why: Juan Jairo Coronilla, a 28-year-old from Guanajuato holding a work visa, was struck by a truck while evading an immigration raid, raising questions about ICE enforcement tactics affecting Mexican nationals.
events.background: Mexico is a country of large-scale emigration to the United States, and enforcement actions by US immigration agents against Mexican nationals recur as a source of friction between the two governments.
events.drivers: Coronilla, in Orlando on a legal work visa and due to return to Mexico on 18 July, was struck by a truck while fleeing an ICE encounter alongside three companions.
events.implications: His death is the fourth in a week tied to ICE activity, two of them Mexican nationals, a toll that has caused outrage in both countries.

events.title: USTR prepares visit to Mexico as USMCA review continues
events.why: The U.S. Trade Representative acknowledged progress on so-called non-tariff barriers ahead of a planned visit to Mexico, part of the ongoing review of the USMCA trade pact.
events.background: USMCA governs Mexico-US-Canada trade and, since the US declined a 16-year extension at the July 2026 joint review, the pact now faces annual reviews through 2036. Economy Secretary Marcelo Ebrard's team has been negotiating with USTR for months on the underlying trade-barrier disputes.
events.drivers: Washington is sending negotiators for a third bilateral round, July 21-23 in Mexico City, to advance talks on steel and aluminum, autos, economic security, labor, agriculture and electronic payment services.
events.implications: Greer says the goal includes closing gaps that let third countries benefit improperly from the treaty, tying the review to non-tariff barriers already flagged in USTR's 2026 report.
events.next: The two delegations meet July 21-23 in Mexico City to continue the sector-by-sector talks.

events.title: BIS warns Banxico and other central banks on excessive debt purchases
events.why: The Bank for International Settlements flagged reputational risk for central banks, including Banxico, that intervene heavily in money markets by buying government debt.
events.background: Banxico sets its policy rate to hold inflation near its 3% target and, like other central banks, sometimes buys government debt in secondary markets to smooth market disruptions. Public debt as a share of GDP has been rising.
events.drivers: The BIS says elevated debt-to-GDP ratios in countries including Mexico complicate managing money supply and interest rates, which can force central banks to intervene more often to fix market dysfunctions.
events.implications: The BIS warns that repeated large-scale asset purchases or credit operations risk creating moral hazard for both financial markets and governments.

events.title: Nu eyes payroll banking to challenge BBVA and Santander in Mexico
events.why: Analysts say Nu Mexico's base of 15 million users gives it an opening to compete for payroll accounts, a segment long dominated by legacy banks.
events.background: Nu México just won approval to operate as a bank, entering a market where BBVA and other legacy banks dominate payroll accounts, the anchor product banks use to lock in other business.
events.implications: Winning payroll clients would strip traditional banks of a cheap financing source tied to their corporate payroll agreements.
events.drivers: Nu points to its 15 million Mexican users to challenge that dominance, and analysts note payroll portability is a worker's right, not the employer's, so better direct terms could shift accounts quickly.

events.title: Business council says USMCA's future hinges on 2026 or 2029 U.S. elections
events.why: CCE leader José Medina Mora made the comments ahead of a third round of negotiations between Mexico and the United States over the trade pact's review.
events.background: USMCA's joint review process, triggered after the U.S. declined a 16-year extension in July 2026, now requires annual reviews under Article 34.7, with the treaty otherwise running through 2036.
events.drivers: CCE president José Medina Mora says the treaty's direction still depends largely on Trump's own position, based on conversations with US agencies ahead of the third negotiating round.
events.next: Mexico and the U.S. begin a third round of USMCA negotiations.
events.implications: The private sector's own timeline shows the annual-review structure could leave USMCA's terms unsettled for years rather than resolved soon.

events.title: Finance ministry misses deadline to issue rules under anti-money-laundering reform
events.why: The 2025 reform was billed as the biggest update to Mexico's anti-laundering regime in over a decade, but Hacienda's delay in issuing implementing rules stalls its application.
events.background: The July 2025 reform to Mexico's anti-money-laundering law, LFPIORPI, took effect in March and was meant to tighten oversight of real estate, vehicle sales, jewelry and other 'vulnerable activities' through new beneficial-owner identification and cash-use rules.
events.drivers: Hacienda has not published the general provisions needed to operationalize several of the law's new obligations, including beneficial-owner identification, cash-use limits and new SAT reporting schemes.
events.implications: The regulatory vacuum leaves the vulnerable-activity sectors the reform targeted without the rules needed to comply in practice.

events.title: 'El Mayo' Zambada awaits life sentence in the United States
events.why: The former Sinaloa cartel leader, over 70, faces sentencing on charges of trafficking tons of drugs into the U.S., a case with weight in the bilateral security relationship.
events.background: Zambada, longtime Sinaloa Cartel leader, was captured in an unusual 2024 arrest and has since been jailed in the United States.
events.next: His defense has asked that, if sentenced to life, he be held in a federal prison with specialized medical care.
events.implications: His defense has asked that, if he receives a life sentence, he be placed in a federal prison offering specialized medical care.

events.title: Baja California governor's US visa restoration draws scrutiny
events.why: Marina del Pilar Ávila regained her U.S. visa after showing willingness to cooperate with Washington, a contrast with the Sheinbaum government's stated defense of sovereignty.
events.background: Trump administration officials have pursued an assertive approach toward Mexico under the banner of fighting 'narcoterrorism,' prompting Sheinbaum's government to make sovereignty defense a top priority and to ask officials and legislators to avoid actions that concede to Washington.
events.drivers: The article contrasts Sheinbaum's sovereignty stance with a governor who has shown willingness to do whatever it takes to win U.S. favor in order to get her visa back.
events.implications: The governor's approach undercuts the unified sovereignty posture the federal government has tried to project toward Washington.

events.title: Mexico extends its venture capital lead over Brazil in Latin America
events.why: Mexican startups raised 944 million dollars in the second quarter, up 131 percent from a year earlier, pulling further ahead of Brazil as global venture firms increase their presence in the region.
events.background: Latin America's venture market has been split mainly between Mexico and Brazil, with global funds increasingly active in the region's fintech, logistics and transportation startups.
events.drivers: Investors including Founders Fund and Andreessen Horowitz led some of the quarter's largest deals, and Mexico-based companies took the region's three biggest fundraising deals in Q2.
events.implications: A continued boom in late-stage and growth funding lifted the whole region, with Latin American startups raising $1.36 billion in the quarter, up 47% year over year.

events.title: Antitrust fine against Liverpool and Gicsa heads to competition courts
events.why: The fine, notified in June and rooted in a 2020 investigation by the former competition regulator, will test how Mexico's competition tribunals handle enforcement cases from that era.
events.background: The National Antimonopoly Commission (CNA) succeeded Cofece as Mexico's competition regulator and inherited pending cases, including a 2020 probe into possible monopolistic practices in commercial real estate leasing.
events.drivers: The fine of more than 500 million pesos lets sanctioned firms, including Fibra Danhos and Grupo Acosta Verde, challenge the ruling before Mexico's two specialized competition courts.
events.implications: The case could test how far Cofece-era investigations reach in current tribunals.
events.next: The dispute could eventually reach Mexico's Supreme Court after the specialized courts rule.

events.title: Volvo, Honda and Toyota diverge on Mexico investment strategy
events.why: Volvo's bet on Mexico stands out against the different paths taken by other automakers, as manufacturers weigh where to place capacity amid shifting trade and tariff conditions.
events.background: Automakers have been recalibrating North American production amid shifting trade and tariff conditions under USMCA, weighing new Mexican capacity against existing U.S. plants.
events.drivers: Volvo's new Ciénega de Flores plant began trial runs in May, and CEO Martin Lundstedt told analysts output there will rise gradually through the second half of the year despite June delivery problems.
events.implications: Volvo will keep covering part of North American demand, including for its Mack Trucks unit, from its Virginia and Pennsylvania plants while the Nuevo León line ramps up.

events.title: Mexico addresses US concerns on deforestation, wastewater and export controls before USMCA round
events.why: Washington acknowledged concrete Mexican action on avocado-linked deforestation, cross-border wastewater and dual-use export controls just before the July 21 talks, easing some non-tariff friction points in the review.

coverage.title: Mexico clears environmental and security hurdles ahead of USMCA review round
coverage.summary: Washington has acknowledged Mexican action on avocado deforestation, cross-border wastewater, and dual-use export controls, clearing the way for the July 21 talks in Mexico City.

events.background: USMCA's review covers both tariff issues and non-tariff friction points like environmental and security standards.
events.drivers: Mexico moved on deforestation-linked avocado exports, cross-border wastewater and dual-use export controls before the July 21 round to clear ground for tougher talks on steel, autos and agriculture.
events.next: Negotiating teams spend three days in Mexico City working through steel and related issues.
events.implications: Washington's acknowledgment of these steps eases some non-tariff friction ahead of the harder negotiations.

events.title: El Mayo Zambada sentenced to life, ordered to forfeit $15 billion
events.why: A New York court sentenced the former Sinaloa Cartel leader after his guilty plea, in a case with continuing implications for US-Mexico security cooperation.
events.background: Zambada, arrested in a disputed 2024 operation, had pleaded guilty months earlier to decades of trafficking cocaine, heroin and fentanyl as a Sinaloa Cartel leader.
events.drivers: Judge Brian Cogan confirmed the life sentence for trafficking cocaine, heroin and, in recent years, fentanyl, following the earlier guilty plea.
events.implications: The sentence puts Zambada in the same position as Guzmán, both former Sinaloa Cartel leaders now imprisoned for life in the United States.

events.title: Hacienda's vehicle leasing tender faces collusion probe
events.why: The tender covers leasing up to 18,000 vehicles for the federal government, and authorities are examining possible collusive bids among contractors.
events.background: SHCP's tender covers vehicle leasing for federal agencies, a recurring type of public contract where bid patterns are checked for signs of coordination among bidders.
events.drivers: An analysis found Casanova Vallejo, Grupo Turbofin and Impulsa Tu Ganancia submitted bids with minimal differences among themselves, priced above the most competitive offers submitted by 11 total bidders.
events.implications: The pattern raises questions about whether the winning bid for a four-year, 90-plus-agency contract reflects genuine competition.
events.next: A decision on the tender is due within days.

events.title: EU accuses CEMEX and rivals of coordinating construction chemical prices
events.why: The European Commission says CEMEX and other firms coordinated price increases in France, Germany, and Spain between 2021 and 2022, a case that could bring fines.
events.background: CEMEX is a Mexican multinational cement producer with major operations in Europe, now facing a European Commission antitrust inquiry into construction chemical additives.
events.drivers: The Commission's preliminary view is that CEMEX and other manufacturers and industry associations coordinated price increases on additives used in cement, concrete and mortar in three markets.
events.implications: The formal notification of the Commission's preliminary view exposes CEMEX to potential fines over the 2021-2022 conduct.

events.title: Sheinbaum defends US relationship but says Mexico won't 'bow its head'
events.why: The president criticized the Trump administration over treatment of migrants and interference in Mexican sovereignty while affirming the diplomatic relationship.
events.background: Mexico's president serves one six-year term and has no reelection; Sheinbaum took office in October 2024 amid recurring friction with Washington over migration, security and sovereignty.
events.drivers: Sheinbaum responded to unauthorized US agency operations inside Mexico and to mistreatment of Mexican migrants reported by her government.
events.implications: The two countries are in a prolonged period of bilateral tension, driven especially by Trump's threats against Mexico under the pretext of fighting "narcoterrorism."

events.title: Mexico's corn imports reach record 23.9 million tonnes in first half of 2026
events.why: The surge, the highest level since 2015, deepens Mexico's reliance on imported grain despite domestic production efforts.

coverage.title: Mexico's grain imports hit a decade-high as import dependence grows
coverage.summary: Grain imports, including corn, reached 23.9 million tonnes in the first half of 2026, the highest level since 2015.

events.background: Corn is a staple food in Mexico, and the country has long relied partly on imports to meet demand even as the government pushes domestic production.
events.drivers: The rise was driven mainly by corn purchases up 8.6%, soybeans up 9.8%, soybean meal up 34.1% and canola up 10.6%, according to GCMA.
events.implications: The imports were worth US$7,581 million, the second-highest first-half total on record, behind only 2022 when global food prices spiked after the Russia-Ukraine war began.

events.title: Mexico receives first locomotives for Maya Train freight service
events.why: The 12 locomotives mark a milestone toward the freight service's planned January 2027 start.
events.background: The Maya Train is a passenger rail project across Mexico's southeast now being extended into freight service, aimed at moving cargo through a region that trails the north's industrial base.
events.drivers: The 12 locomotives mark the shift from civil works to operational preparation and are already being used to haul construction materials as the project readies for commercial freight.
events.next: Commercial freight operations are due to begin in January 2027, and the locomotives are already being used to haul construction materials in the meantime.

events.title: Mexico to regulate mobile phone use in schools nationwide
events.why: The federal government will present legislation following nationwide consultations with educators, parents, and experts.
events.drivers: The federal government held nationwide consultations with educators, parents, and experts before drafting the legislation, aiming to balance digital learning against concerns over cyberbullying, mental health, and online safety.
events.background: Mexico has no existing national rule on mobile phone use in schools, leaving the matter to individual states and schools until now.
events.next: The federal government will present legislation to set guidelines on device use among minors.

events.title: USTR pushes for stricter USMCA auto rules of origin ahead of review
events.why: Jamieson Greer proposed tightening rules of origin for the auto industry as trade talks with Mexico continue this week, a change that would affect how much Mexican-made vehicle content qualifies for tariff-free treatment.
events.background: Under Article 34.7, the US, Mexico and Canada now hold annual reviews of USMCA after the US declined a 16-year extension in July, with the treaty running through 2036 unless withdrawn or extended; auto rules of origin determine how much Mexican-made vehicle content qualifies for tariff-free treatment.
events.drivers: Trump, Sheinbaum and Canada's Mark Carney met cordially at the World Cup final even as the US pushed to tighten auto rules of origin ahead of the bilateral round.
events.next: The third round of bilateral negotiations on the joint USMCA review runs from Tuesday July 21 to Friday July 24 in Mexico City.
events.implications: Coverage cited in the article notes the annual reviews have already dealt a psychological blow to private investment in Mexico.

events.title: Trump approves 50% tariff on Canada amid USMCA negotiations
events.why: The tariff targets autos, dairy and alcohol over what Trump calls discriminatory treatment of US products, adding pressure to the parallel USMCA review that includes Mexico.
events.background: USMCA trade now proceeds under annual reviews rather than a long extension, after the US declined to renew the pact for 16 years on July 1.
events.drivers: Trump says the tariffs offset what he calls discriminatory treatment of US products by Canada, covering motor vehicle materials, dairy and alcohol.
events.implications: The tariffs were announced the same day the second round of the trilateral USMCA review began.
events.next: The Trump administration said the tariffs take effect in 30 days.

events.title: US-Mexico wage gap becomes a friction point in USMCA review
events.why: A Baker Institute study found US workers earn nearly ten times more than their Mexican counterparts, adding pressure on Mexico ahead of the treaty's review.

coverage.title: US-Mexico wage gap becomes a friction point in USMCA review
coverage.summary: A Baker Institute study found US workers earn nearly ten times more than their Mexican counterparts, adding pressure on Mexico ahead of the treaty's review.

events.background: USMCA reviews now happen annually after the US declined a 16-year extension in 2026, and wage and labor standards are part of the talks alongside tariffs and rules of origin.
events.drivers: The Baker Institute study attributes the gap to limited union bargaining power rather than skill differences, and links it to informality that reached 55.2% in May 2026.

events.title: Pemex reports oil leak and fire at Dos Bocas refinery
events.why: The company said firefighting personnel extinguished the flame with no injuries reported, the latest safety incident at Mexico's flagship refinery project.

coverage.title: Pemex reports oil leak and fire at Dos Bocas refinery
coverage.summary: The company said standard safety procedures contained the fire with no injuries reported, at a refinery central to the government's fuel self-sufficiency plans.

events.background: Dos Bocas is Pemex's flagship refinery, built to cover domestic gasoline and diesel demand but plagued by cost overruns and delays.
events.implications: Pemex said the refinery remained in normal operating condition after the fire, with no risk to personnel, the community or the environment.
events.drivers: A leak in an oil loading pump caused fire and black smoke, prompting Pemex to apply operating procedures and extinguish the flame.
